title: Omalotheca diminuta
taxonomy: Omalotheca diminuta is a species of succulent plant in the family Crassulaceae. It belongs to the genus Omalotheca, which is a group of small to medium-sized succulents. Omalotheca diminuta is further classified as a member of the subgenus Sedum, which includes a diverse range of succulent species. The plant is native to Europe and Western Asia, and is often found growing in dry, rocky areas. As a member of the Crassulaceae family, Omalotheca diminuta is closely related to other succulent plants such as Sedum and Sempervivum.

The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to promote digestive health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
